All confirmed as real code, no comments/strings.

## `eloop_remove_timeout()` — real code references under `src/`

Two independent `static` functions share this name (one per platform backend); each is local to its file.

**`src/utils/eloop.c`** (POSIX backend — active build, LSP-verified 5 refs)
- L652: `static void eloop_remove_timeout(struct eloop_timeout *timeout)` — definition
- L674: call inside `eloop_remove_timeout(timeout);` (removal-matching loop)
- L702: call inside `eloop_remove_timeout(timeout);` (removal-matching loop)
- L1008: call inside `eloop_remove_timeout(timeout);` (timeout-expiry processing)
- L1084: call inside `eloop_remove_timeout(timeout);` (cleanup path)

**`src/utils/eloop_win.c`** (Windows backend — not part of this build's `compile_commands.json`, so clangd doesn't index it; verified via `hover`/grep instead of `findReferences`)
- L285: `static void eloop_remove_timeout(struct eloop_timeout *timeout)` — definition
- L305: call
- L333: call
- L594: call
- L656: call

Total: **10 real references** (2 definitions + 8 call sites) across these two files. No occurrences appear inside string literals or comments — grep across all of `src/` found no other matches (e.g., no hits in `eloop.h`, log messages, or doc comments).
